stem: A agricultural research study surveys 20 seeds planted and observes 10 positive outcomes out of 20 (50.0%). Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample proportion: 0.5. Wilson 95% CI: [0.2993, 0.7007]. Point estimate: 0.5.

common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
